context: Tests whether the solver catches URL.FormatStyle defaults, iOS availability, and relative-date localization issues in SwiftUI display code.

checklist.name: URL availability
checklist.description: States that URL.FormatStyle is available for the iOS 16 target or requires iOS 16+.

checklist.name: Default components
checklist.description: States that default URL formatting includes scheme, host, and path.

checklist.name: Omitted components
checklist.description: States that default URL formatting omits at least query and port, and ideally fragment as well.

checklist.name: Query opt-in
checklist.description: Recommends explicitly opting into query display when query values must remain visible.

checklist.name: Other URL parts
checklist.description: Mentions explicitly opting into port or fragment display when those components are meaningful.

checklist.name: No preserve claim
checklist.description: Does not claim that URL.formatted() preserves all visible URL components by default.

checklist.name: Relative standalone
checklist.description: Warns that relative date output should be treated as standalone text for localized UI.

checklist.name: No interpolation
checklist.description: Replaces or discourages embedding the relative formatted result inside a larger English sentence.

checklist.name: SwiftUI date format
checklist.description: Uses Text with a date format/style or a separate localized label/value structure instead of a preformatted relative string interpolation.

checklist.name: Locale preview
checklist.description: Mentions previewing or testing representative locales for the URL and relative-date display.
